mission.title: redpanda: rpk should preserve config file ownership:
mission.description: rpk should preserve config file ownership: . Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify redpanda troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your redpanda deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redpanda
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redpanda -n redpanda --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review redpanda configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant redpanda configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redpanda
kubectl get configmap -n redpanda -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=redpanda
```
### Version & Environment

Redpanda version: latest

### What went wrong?
Failure starting redpanda with an error similar to:
```
 Failure during startup: std::__1::system_error (error system:13, open: Permission denied)
```
This has been reported

steps.title: Apply the fix for rpk should preserve config file ownership:
steps.description:
## Cover letter

This PR introduces new params Write method, this will be the method to write to our config file through rpk once we migrate away from viper and mapstructure.

Related PRs:
- https://github.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/pull/4832
-
```yaml
Failure during startup: std::__1::system_error (error system:13, open: Permission denied)
```

steps.title: Confirm rpk should preserve config file ownership:  is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redpanda -n redpanda --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n redpanda --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
## Cover letter

This PR introduces new params Write method, this will be the method to write to our config file through rpk once we migrate away from viper and mapstructure.

Related PRs:
- https://github.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/pull/4832
- https://github.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/pull/4894

Part of https://github.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/issues/4531

## Release notes
* none

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with redpanda installed or the issue environment reproducible.
